Darius.trading_pattern: Avoid extended trades at all costs — Darius's bleed prevents your passive regen. Look for short Q-E-W trades only when he has no E available, then back off immediately.
Darius.ability_to_dodge: Darius Q — the outer ring does the most damage; hug him tightly or stay far back to avoid the full hit.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (execute threat), Sunfire Cape first item
power_spikes.enemy: Level 5 (full bleed stacks), Stridebreaker first item

Darius.early_game: Start Doran's Shield and play defensively. Don't let him stack bleed — disengage after each trade. Farm safely and wait for jungle pressure.
Darius.mid_game: Avoid 1v1s unless you're ahead. Shove and rotate; you lose most side lane duels once he has Stridebreaker.
Darius.late_game: Teamfights are your friend — his ult only hits one target but yours executes the lowest HP target. Peel away from him in fights.
Darius.win_condition: Get early jungle help to deny his lane dominance. Rush Steelcaps + Bramble Vest to reduce his bleed sustain. Win via objective play, not dueling.
Darius.scaling: Darius favored throughout — Garen slightly better in late teamfights but loses sidelane all game
Darius.positioning: Don't stand in the outer ring of his Q. Hug the wave so minions chip him when he engages. Never let him proc 5 bleed stacks.

Fiora.trading_pattern: Short trades with Q-E-W only. She will Riposte your Q silence, so delay Q slightly after she uses W. She wins extended trades due to Vitals healing.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora W (Riposte) — if she uses it proactively, wait it out before using Q. Never walk into her when W is available.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (max Riposte cooldown reduction), Ravenous Hydra

Fiora.early_game: She can't all-in you easily at level 1-2, so farm and poke with Q. Once she hits level 3, respect her full kit. Don't let her proc 2+ Vitals in one trade.
Fiora.mid_game: You lose most duels after she has Hydra. Shove wave and go to teamfights — she scales better in split push.
Fiora.late_game: Avoid her in side lane entirely. Group with your team; she's strong 1v1 but less threatening in 5v5s unless she can isolate carries.
Fiora.win_condition: Win before she gets two items. Use ignite to cancel her Vital healing. Get your jungler to gank early and often to deny her CS.
Fiora.scaling: Fiora outscales significantly — she wins all duels past 2 items
Fiora.positioning: Keep track of which direction her active Vital is — reposition to deny it. Stand to the side of her, never directly in front.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: Walk to the sides of his Q sweetspots to dodge the knockup. Trade during his Q animation windows when he's committed. Your W negates a chunk of his burst.
Aatrox.ability_to_dodge: Aatrox Q — sidestep the outer edge zones; standing between the two Q zones avoids most damage.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (Q cooldown reduction), Goredrinker / Eclipse first item

Aatrox.early_game: Farm and short trade. Walk to the safe zone of his Q and punish him when he misses. Avoid full combos — his passive revive at level 1 is tricky.
Aatrox.mid_game: You can match him in side lane if ahead. Respect his ult healing; don't fight him when it's up unless you can burst him before he casts it.
Aatrox.late_game: He outscales in duels but you're better in teamfights. Stick to mid and support objectives.
Aatrox.win_condition: Get an early kill to deny his snowball. Build Bramble Vest second to reduce his healing. Win through objective control rather than dueling.
Aatrox.scaling: Aatrox outscales in side lane; even in teamfights
Aatrox.positioning: Stay between his Q hitboxes — the safe zone is just inside each Q circle edge. Don't stand directly in front of him.

Camille.trading_pattern: She wins all extended trades with her passive shield and E true damage. Only short Q-E-W trades when she has no E available. Disengage before her W heal triggers.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E (Hookshot) — if she misses, punish immediately. Pre-position away from walls so she can't get a clean angle.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (full combo available), Trinity Force first item

Camille.early_game: She outtraded you levels 1-5. Farm under tower if needed. Conqueror into her means you must look for extended trades but her kit prevents it.
Camille.mid_game: She outduels you and can use ult to isolate you 1v1. Don't fight near walls. Consider teleporting to other fights.
Camille.late_game: She dives your carries — peel them or die to her ult isolation. She's a bigger threat in side lane all game.
Camille.win_condition: Need jungle assistance early. Play for teleport plays and team fighting where her ult isolation is less impactful.
Camille.scaling: Camille outscales in duels all game; Garen slightly better in pure teamfight chaos
Camille.positioning: Stay away from walls — she needs wall geometry for her E angle. Hug the center of the lane.

Jax.trading_pattern: Abuse the early levels before he has E — trade hard at level 1 and 2. Once he has Counterstrike (E), only trade while E is on cooldown. Never trade into his E.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: Jax E (Counterstrike) — don't autoattack him while it's spinning. Trade with Q and E then back off during his E stun.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult passive stacks), Trinity Force + Steelcaps

Jax.early_game: Play extremely aggressive at level 1-2 before he has E. Force him to burn flash or take heavy damage. Back off once he hits level 3.
Jax.mid_game: He starts winning trades after Trinity Force. Match his shove and roam; avoid prolonged 1v1s.
Jax.late_game: He outscales hard. Group and fight in teamfights — his 1v1 is far stronger than yours late game.
Jax.win_condition: Snowball the early levels 1-2 advantage into a kill or major CS lead. Keep him behind before Trinity Force comes online.
Jax.scaling: Jax outscales significantly past first item — Garen only wins early
Jax.positioning: Stand at max Q range to proc it without walking into his E. Don't autoattack when his E is glowing.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: Short trades with Q before he gets fury stacked. His crits become devastating — never fight him at full fury. W before his spinning slash hits.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere E (Spinning Slash) — he uses it to gap close and escape. Pre-W when you see him wind up an E-Q trade.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult makes him unkillable), Phantom Drive first item

Tryndamere.early_game: Abuse him pre-6 when he can't make fights 'free'. Poke with Q and deny CS. He can't really all-in you before ult.
Tryndamere.mid_game: He starts winning duels after first crit item. His split push becomes oppressive — you must match him or he takes towers.
Tryndamere.late_game: Never fight him 1v1 in side lane. Group for objectives. His ult makes 1v1 kills impossible during the timer.
Tryndamere.win_condition: Get ahead early and roam to snowball other lanes. You can't reliably kill him at 6+ in side lane — win by objective control.
Tryndamere.scaling: Tryndamere outscales hard — Garen only relevant early
Tryndamere.positioning: Deny his fury generation by not standing near him at low fury. Kite sideways during his E so he can't immediately follow up.

Vayne.trading_pattern: All-in early before she gets items — she can't escape your E. But her Silver Bolts true damage shreds you in extended fights. Short all-ins only.
Vayne.ability_to_dodge: Vayne E (Condemn) — never stand near walls or she'll stun you. Stay center of lane at all times.

power_spikes.enemy: Guinsoo's Rageblade first item, Level 6 (ult stealth)

Vayne.early_game: She's squishy and vulnerable early. Charge her with Q and try to all-in at level 2-3. Deny her CS with your threat.
Vayne.mid_game: Her Silver Bolts start melting your HP. She becomes very hard to kill and can kite your E. Respect her dueling power.
Vayne.late_game: She counters you fundamentally — true damage bypasses your W. Avoid 1v1s entirely. In teamfights, try to land E on her early to delete her.
Vayne.win_condition: Kill her early or force her off the wave. If you can't kill her before Guinsoo's, roam and win elsewhere. Ignite to deny her potential lifesteal.
Vayne.scaling: Vayne outscales hard — Garen only wins very early
Vayne.positioning: Keep her away from walls to prevent Condemn stuns. Never chase her along wall edges.

Kayle.trading_pattern: All-in windows: level 2, level 5, level 6 if you hit it first, and level 9. Outside of these windows she kites you permanently. Charge in with Q and force all-ins.
Kayle.ability_to_dodge: Kayle E (Starfire Spellblade) — her ranged empowered autos hurt a lot; engage when she just used E on a minion.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 11 (fully ranged and untouchable), Nashor's Tooth + Rabadon's

Kayle.early_game: Abuse her hard before level 6. She's melee and squishy at levels 1-5. Take Ignite and try to kill her at level 2 with Q-E combo.
Kayle.mid_game: By level 11 she's fully ranged and you literally cannot reach her. Roam mid or group; staying top is a losing proposition.
Kayle.late_game: She's untouchable with her ult and ranged form. Don't attempt to fight her. Win the game through other lanes.
Kayle.win_condition: Kill her before level 6, ideally twice. If you can't get a kill early, ask for jungle pressure and roam. She wins the game if she's left alone.
Kayle.scaling: Kayle outscales astronomically — Garen only wins levels 1-5
Kayle.positioning: Force her under tower early so she loses CS. Don't let her farm safely — she needs levels more than most champions.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: Never fight inside his W passive zone — it gives him bonus stats. Poke with Q and back off. He wins prolonged trades due to his shield generation.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser R (Realm of Death) — when he ults you, Flash out if possible, otherwise W immediately and fight. Never panic in the realm.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult dueling), Riftmaker first item

Mordekaiser.early_game: He builds shield fast with his passive. Don't trade into his E slow. Farm and harass with Q but don't commit to extended fights.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: He outduels you once Riftmaker is online. Match his shove and roam. Don't fight him in 1v1 scenarios.
Mordekaiser.late_game: His ult is deadly for isolating carries. Peel your backline. His teamfight isn't as strong as his dueling.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Get ahead early and build Grievous Wounds to cut his Riftmaker healing. Win through objective play, not dueling.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Mordekaiser outscales in duels; even in teamfights
Mordekaiser.positioning: Don't cluster inside his W passive range. Stand outside the golden ring to deny his passive procs.

Irelia.trading_pattern: Only fight her when her passive has 0-2 stacks. At max stacks she destroys you. Keep minions low HP near you to prevent her from resetting Q on them.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia E (Flawless Duet) — sidestep the two blades; if they both hit you they stun and she wins the trade completely.

power_spikes.you: Level 2-4 (before she maxes passive stacks), Level 6
power_spikes.enemy: Level 5 (max passive stacks accessible), Trinity Force first item

Irelia.early_game: Freeze the wave away from her tower so she can't stack passive on minions. Fight her at 0 stacks. She can't reliably all-in early if you respect her E.
Irelia.mid_game: She outduels you with Trinity and max passive. Don't fight 1v1. Shove and roam.
Irelia.late_game: Her teamfight is strong. Peel carries and use your E on her if she dives in.
Irelia.win_condition: Deny her passive stacks by keeping wave off her. Poke with Q when she has 0 stacks. Get kills early or it becomes unwinnable.
Irelia.scaling: Irelia outscales in duels past first item
Irelia.positioning: Stand between the two E blades' expected path to sidestep. Keep low HP minions away from her so she can't stack passive.

Renekton.trading_pattern: Never fight at level 3 — he wins decisively with Q-W-E combo and fury. Wait until levels 6+ when your ult threat evens the lane. Use W to absorb his W stun.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton W (Ruthless Predator) — when you see him jump with E, immediately W to reduce the stun/damage. Never trade while W is down.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult threat and stats), Sunfire Cape
power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (full combo), Level 9 (Q max), Black Cleaver

Renekton.early_game: He dominates levels 1-6 hard. Farm and survive. Use W proactively when you see E incoming. Don't fight without jungle backup.
Renekton.mid_game: He falls off mid-game relative to early. Match his shove — you can fight more even footing once you have Sunfire.
Renekton.late_game: Your teamfight is stronger. He's best as a lane bully — his late game isn't impressive. Group and win.
Renekton.win_condition: Survive the early game, get jungle help, and out-scale. Buy Plated Steelcaps ASAP. His power fades after level 10-12.
Renekton.scaling: Renekton favored early, even mid, Garen slightly better late
Renekton.positioning: Don't stand in the center of lane where he can E in from either side. Hug the side closest to your tower for easier retreat.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Harass him constantly with Q every time he goes for CS. He has no early damage without stacks. Don't let him free farm — every denied Q stack matters.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W (Wither) — don't chase him into it; it will slow you to zero and he can kite or turret dive with his ult.

power_spikes.enemy: 300+ Q stacks, Level 6 (ult), Frozen Heart + Hullbreaker

Nasus.early_game: Be aggressive and deny stacks. Every Q he gets on a canon minion is a problem late game. Push and deny CS with E-Q poke.
Nasus.mid_game: Once he has Frozen Heart and stacks he becomes hard to kill. Don't fight him at 6 unless you can burst before ult.
Nasus.late_game: 500+ stacks Nasus can kill you in 2-3 Qs. Avoid side lane duels late game. Force objectives while he's split pushing.
Nasus.win_condition: Deny as many Q stacks as possible early. Win the game before he hits 400 stacks. Push and roam to snowball other lanes.
Nasus.scaling: Nasus outscales massively — Garen wins early, Nasus wins past 20 minutes
Nasus.positioning: Stand between him and the CS he's trying to last-hit with Q. Force him to choose between taking poke or getting stacks.

Sett.trading_pattern: Avoid his W (Haymaker) — if he has full grit, back off immediately. He wins W-empowered trades decisively. Short Q-E trades work well.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W (Haymaker) — the true damage center is extremely deadly; sidestep to avoid the center of the explosion when he throws it.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult grab), Heartsteel + Hullbreaker

Sett.early_game: Fairly even. Don't let him auto you too freely — he generates grit from taking damage. Short trades while his W grit is low.
Sett.mid_game: He wins 1v1s once Heartsteel is up. Shove and roam. Match his teleport pressure.
Sett.late_game: His teamfight initiations are powerful. Stay out of his ult grab range. You're both better in brawls but he's stronger.
Sett.win_condition: Get ahead early and deny his grit generation. Rush Bramble Vest to reduce his auto-attack trading power. Win through objective play.
Sett.positioning: Don't stand still while he's auto attacking — move sideways to reduce grit accumulation. Stay away from his W explosion center.

Malphite.trading_pattern: Charge Q and E on him repeatedly. He has no sustain and takes big damage from your E. His armor scaling makes trades less effective late but you win early.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite Q (Seismic Shard) — it applies a movement slow; avoid face-tanking it by staying at max range and sidestepping slightly.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Unstoppable Force), Sunfire Aegis + Randuin's Omen

Malphite.early_game: Abuse him heavily before he gets armor items. Your E ignores armor via its spin damage and you out-trade him at all pre-6 levels.
Malphite.mid_game: His armor makes your physical damage less effective. Build Black Cleaver if ahead. He'll roam for ult combos — push and take plates.
Malphite.late_game: His ult is a game-changer in teamfights — be ready to W when he lands on your team. You win sidelane duels.
Malphite.win_condition: Get a big early lead and deny his gold income. Push your advantage before he completes his armor items.
Malphite.scaling: Garen favored early; Malphite better in teamfights late via ult
Malphite.positioning: Don't let him chunk you with Q at range. Stand outside Q range and all-in when he walks up to CS.

Olaf.trading_pattern: He ignores slows with his ult and can't be CC'd. Your W silence is useless against him. He wins extended trades with lifesteal and axes. Only fight when his axes are on cooldown.
Olaf.ability_to_dodge: Olaf Q (Undertow) — dodge the axe throw. He needs to walk to pick it up, so bait him into throwing it away from him.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult, CC immunity), Stridebreaker first item

Olaf.early_game: His axes hurt early. Don't try to match him in extended trades. Farm safely and call for jungle assistance.
Olaf.mid_game: He's hard to stop once he has Stridebreaker and ult. Shove and rotate — don't duel him.
Olaf.late_game: His ult into your team is devastating. Try to poke him before he activates ult. Your execute is useful if he goes low.
Olaf.win_condition: Get jungle help early. Survive his power window and wait for a gank. Your ult execute threat prevents him from ignoring you at low HP.
Olaf.scaling: Olaf favored throughout; Garen slightly better in pure execute scenarios
Olaf.positioning: Stand to the side of his axe throw trajectory. Force him to retrieve axe in a bad position.

Riven.trading_pattern: She wins short trades with her shield and mobility. Trade only when her shield (E) is on cooldown. Use W silence to interrupt her combo mid-sequence.
Riven.ability_to_dodge: Riven E (Valor/Shield) — track its cooldown. When it's down she's vulnerable. Her entire kit becomes less effective without the shield.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (max CDR on combo), Black Cleaver + Ravenous Hydra

Riven.early_game: Fairly even at level 1. Be aggressive and match her trade at level 2. Don't let her land full 3-Q combo — move between each Q.
Riven.mid_game: She outduels with full build. Match her shove and avoid straight 1v1s.
Riven.late_game: Her execute ult timing can delete your carries. Your W silence disrupts her burst if timed right.
Riven.win_condition: Punish her when E is down. Take Ignite for early kill pressure. Win the game before she completes two damage items.
Riven.scaling: Riven outscales in duels; Garen better in teamfight brawls
Riven.positioning: Move sideways between each of her Q casts to reduce Q3 knockup reliability. Don't stand still.

Shen.trading_pattern: Bait out his W (Spirit's Refuge) before committing to a full trade. He wins trades during his dodge window. Hit Q before he activates W.
Shen.ability_to_dodge: Shen W (Spirit's Refuge) — don't autoattack or use E during the dodge zone. Q him instead since it's not a basic attack.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (global ult), Sunfire Aegis + Heartsteel

Shen.early_game: You out-trade him early. Spam Q and E to deny his passive proc. Keep him off the wave to prevent energy regen.
Shen.mid_game: Respect his global ult — push wave then back when it's available so he can't ult and win a fight elsewhere while you're overextended.
Shen.late_game: His teamfight is good. Don't autoattack into his W zone. You can brawl with him but he has better utility.
Shen.win_condition: Build early CS lead and roam. Track his ult cooldown and push objectives during its downtime.
Shen.scaling: Even — both fall into teamfight utility roles late
Shen.positioning: Step out of his W dodge zone when he activates it. Retreat the sword slightly then re-engage after the window expires.

Sion.trading_pattern: Interrupt his Q charge with your Q silence — if he's charging Q, sprint at him and Q to cancel it. Short trades win for you.
Sion.ability_to_dodge: Sion Q (Decimating Smash) — run toward him perpendicular to his charge direction. Don't run directly away as you'll get hit by the knockup.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult roam), Sunfire + Heartsteel (passive HP stacks)

Sion.early_game: You out-trade him significantly early. He can't really fight you if you interrupt his Q. Shove hard and deny HP stacks.
Sion.mid_game: His roam ult is dangerous — push and create pressure rather than following him blindly.
Sion.late_game: His HP becomes massive late game. Your execute hits harder on high-HP targets. Land ult on Sion for a satisfying delete.
Sion.win_condition: Deny his Heartsteel passive stacks by trading and winning early. Build your own lead and roam with advantage.
Sion.scaling: Even — Garen slightly better early, Sion better late via HP scaling
Sion.positioning: Move toward him at an angle when his Q is charging to reduce travel time and cancel it with Q.

Trundle.trading_pattern: Avoid standing in his W (Frozen Domain) — it gives him massive attack speed and movement speed. Draw him off his pillar before trading.
Trundle.ability_to_dodge: Trundle E (Pillar of Ice) — it blocks paths; don't get cornered against it. He'll use it to slow you and keep you in his W zone.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult steals resistances), Sunfire Aegis + Sterak's

Trundle.early_game: You win early trades significantly. Harass with Q constantly. Don't let him free farm to enable his ult pillar plays.
Trundle.mid_game: His ult stealing your armor and MR is massive. Avoid 1v1s at 6+. Roam to where you're more useful.
Trundle.late_game: His ult on a tanky frontliner is devastating. Keep your distance in teamfights — don't be the one he chains ult on.
Trundle.win_condition: Snowball early lead before his ult and items come online. Get kills and roam — once he's behind, his ult is less impactful.
Trundle.scaling: Trundle outscales in 1v1s; Garen better in 5v5
Trundle.positioning: Never fight while standing in his W zone. Sidestep his pillar immediately when it drops.

Urgot.trading_pattern: He wins almost all trades with his passive shotgun knees empowered autos. Only fight when he's out of passive stacks. His W passive makes him reduce your damage.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot E (Disdain) — if he runs at you with shield up, respect the engage. His E grab is point-click — don't get too close.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (execute ult), Frozen Fist + Sunfire

Urgot.early_game: He out-trades you at all stages with his passive. Play under tower and farm. His range means you can't freely Q him.
Urgot.mid_game: He wins 1v1s after first item. Match his shove and roam where you're more effective.
Urgot.late_game: His teamfight ult execute is terrifying. In teamfights, watch for his ult on carries. You can sometimes race-condition ult him if he's sub-40% HP.
Urgot.win_condition: Need jungle presence to survive. Take Ignite for kill threat. Win through team play — he dominates side lane.
Urgot.scaling: Urgot outscales in duels; Garen slightly better in group fights
Urgot.positioning: Stay outside his E range (short-medium). Don't stand in front of his knees — angle so you take fewer passive shots.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Kill his ghouls quickly — they deal significant damage if left alive. Short trades work; avoid trading while his Maiden is active as it adds significant DPS.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick E (Mourning Mist) — it marks you for Maiden and Ghoul attacks; sidestep it to avoid the empowered harassment.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Maiden summon), Hullbreaker + Sunfire

Yorick.early_game: Pretty even early. Kill ghouls with E so he can't last-hit with them. Deny him Mist charges to delay Maiden.
Yorick.mid_game: His split push with Maiden is dangerous. You have to match him in side lane or he takes towers. He wins 1v1 with Maiden.
Yorick.late_game: His Maiden and ghouls can siege and backdoor. Match his split or send someone else while you group.
Yorick.win_condition: Deny Mist charges by killing his ghouls and standing away from his E. If you can deny Maiden long enough, your early lead matters.
Yorick.scaling: Yorick outscales via split push; even in direct fights
Yorick.positioning: Don't let ghouls free-hit you. Clear them with E spin. Sidestep his E to deny Maiden empowerment.

Jayce.trading_pattern: He wins all poke trades with hammer-cannon combo. Look to all-in when he's in cannon form — he's weaker up close. Use Q to close the gap on his cannon form.
Jayce.ability_to_dodge: Jayce Q (Shock Blast / Thundering Blow) — in cannon form, dodge his Q arc; in hammer form, dodge the knockback by sidestepping.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 7 (Q max in cannon), Trinity Force first item

Jayce.early_game: He chunks you at range constantly. Farm with Doran's Shield and Fleet Footwork. Don't overextend. Call for jungle help.
Jayce.mid_game: Once he has Trinity he roams and dominates. Push wave fast and match his roams with teleport.
Jayce.late_game: He falls off a bit — his teamfight is primarily poke damage. Your W reduces burst if you engage on him.
Jayce.win_condition: Force all-ins in his hammer form. Take Flash-Ignite and look for a kill when he's melee. Get jungle help to punish his squishy early game.
Jayce.scaling: Jayce favored early-mid; Garen better in brawls
Jayce.positioning: Hug minions to reduce his cannon Q poke angles. Force him to be in melee range to CS.

Gnar.trading_pattern: In mini form, only trade when he's thrown his boomerang. In mega form, W his ult stun and go ham. His mega form is actually disadvantaged vs you.
Gnar.ability_to_dodge: Gnar Q (Boomerang Throw) — sidestep it on the way out; it returns and can hit twice. Don't stand in a straight line from him.

power_spikes.enemy: Mega form rage stacks, Trinity Force + Heartsteel

Gnar.early_game: He pokes you down from range safely in mini form. Farm under tower with Doran's Shield. Don't chase him at range.
Gnar.mid_game: Respect mega form — it's a huge power window but then he's back to mini. Fight during mini form when he's low rage.
Gnar.late_game: His GNAR! ult into walls is devastating in teamfights. Peel from walls and don't cluster near terrain.
Gnar.win_condition: Wait out his mega rage and punish the transition back. Get jungle help to catch him in mini form. Your execute hurts his high-HP mega form.
Gnar.scaling: Gnar outscales in teamfights via GNAR! utility; even in 1v1
Gnar.positioning: Don't stand near walls when he's in mega form — GNAR! wall bounce is how he wins teamfights. Stay in open areas.

Illaoi.trading_pattern: If her E misses, go in immediately — she's weak without it. Never fight while tentacles are up. Short Q trades between E cooldowns.
Illaoi.ability_to_dodge: Illaoi E (Test of Spirit) — this is everything. Dodge it or the fight is over. Sidestep and watch her telegraph the throw.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult summons tentacles), Sunfire + Hullbreaker

Illaoi.early_game: You win early before she gets tentacles set up. Harass with Q and deny her E. Don't let her plant tentacles near your half of the wave.
Illaoi.mid_game: Her tentacles make trading into her suicidal. Build Grievous Wounds. Fight only in open areas without tentacles.
Illaoi.late_game: She's a powerful split pusher. Match her or she takes towers. Her ult in a chokepoint is devastating.
Illaoi.win_condition: Dodge her E consistently. Build Bramble Vest to reduce tentacle healing. Win before tentacles are everywhere.
Illaoi.scaling: Illaoi outscales in split push; even in open teamfights
Illaoi.positioning: Never stand directly in front of her during her E wind-up animation. Move laterally.

Varus.trading_pattern: He pokes you down from extreme range and you have no gap close except Q. Wait for him to waste Q then run him down. His W stacks are a problem for extended trades.
Varus.ability_to_dodge: Varus Q (Piercing Arrow) — dodge the charged shot by moving laterally; it's a slow projectile when fully charged.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (execute if he's low), Sunfire Cape
power_spikes.enemy: Kraken Slayer + Guinsoo's (W stacks), Level 6 (ult CC)

Varus.early_game: He pokes freely from range. Doran's Shield and Fleet Footwork. Try to all-in when his Q is charging and he's committed.
Varus.mid_game: His W blight stacks will melt your HP. Once he has Kraken Slayer his true damage hurts through W.
Varus.late_game: His ult chains your whole team. Flank from a side angle to deny his ult line. Your W won't help vs his damage.
Varus.win_condition: You need jungle help to close the gap. He's very kitable — find flanks to get on him. He's squishy early, punish hard with ignite.
Varus.scaling: Varus outscales significantly in ranged kite pattern
Varus.positioning: Hug minions to reduce his poke angles. Approach from diagonal to avoid his Q line.

Yasuo.trading_pattern: Your Q silence prevents him from using Wind Wall, E, or R for its duration. Time Q when he's in an unfavorable position. Don't chase him through minions.
Yasuo.ability_to_dodge: Yasuo Q (Steel Tempest) — the tornado is telegraphed. Sidestep when he's on third stack with a glow on his sword.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult air combo), Infinity Edge + Kraken Slayer

Yasuo.early_game: Fairly even. He needs crit to really hurt you. Your silence disrupts his combos. Trade when his E is on cooldown.
Yasuo.mid_game: He duels you with crits. Your W is massive here — use it to tank his ult combo. Build Plated Steelcaps.
Yasuo.late_game: His crits hurt but your W negates a chunk. In teamfights your E does great damage. Keep him away from your backline.
Yasuo.win_condition: Deny him early crits to slow his snowball. W his ult combo and then fight back. Silence prevents his E-Q dash combos.
Yasuo.scaling: Even early; Yasuo slightly better with full crit build
Yasuo.positioning: Don't fight near minion waves where he can E between minions to dodge. Force open field fights.

Yone.trading_pattern: Similar to Yasuo. Your Q silence prevents his E soul dash return combo. Punish after he uses E — he's committed during the dash.
Yone.ability_to_dodge: Yone E (Soul Unbound) — when he dashes forward, he'll return to his body. Trade hard and then disengage to reset before he returns.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult), Kraken Slayer + Infinity Edge

Yone.early_game: Even matchup early. He has better poke with Q1-Q2-Q3 but your Q silence disrupts him. Short trades favor you.
Yone.mid_game: His damage ramps with items. W into his ult combo then fight back. Silence prevents his ult return.
Yone.late_game: His ult in teamfights is devastating. Use W to survive his burst window then all-in.
Yone.win_condition: Silence his combo at the right time. Get early kills before his items come online. Your execute is reliable on a high-damage dealer who overextends.
Yone.scaling: Even early; Yone outscales in sustained fights
Yone.positioning: When he uses E, back away slightly — when he returns to his body he takes bonus damage based on damage dealt. Trade then disengage during his E window.

Galio.trading_pattern: Poke with Q and E consistently. His passive shield requires him to take magic damage to proc — your mostly physical kit makes the shield harder to use.
Galio.ability_to_dodge: Galio W (Shield of Durand) — if he taunts you, immediately W to reduce the damage and move away from his Q center.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (global ult), Abyssal Mask + Sunfire

Galio.early_game: You out-trade him solidly early. His passive needs magic damage to activate. Your all-physical trading bypasses his passive shield often.
Galio.mid_game: He roams with global ult. Push wave and deny him free roams. Your teleport can match his ult on other lanes.
Galio.late_game: His ult is incredible for engaging teamfights. Peel and use W when he taunts. Your execute on his high-HP build hurts.
Galio.win_condition: Build an early lane lead and roam before he gets his ult items. Track his ult CD and push mid when it's down.
Galio.scaling: Even — Galio better teamfight, Garen better 1v1
Galio.positioning: Stay out of his W taunt range. Don't stand still when he winds up W.
